recommendation.summary: haxe.rust is viable for the current codexhx helper, credential-free headless, runtime/TUI persistence-boundary, native SQLite persistence/state-adapter/read-view/thread-read projection/pagination/active-turn-merge/turn-items unsupported-runtime/token-usage replay/goal-continuation admission/goal-runtime restore/active-turn and budget-limit goal steering/active-goal and idle-goal progress accounting/turn-start accounting/turn finalization/turn-error active-goal stop/goal token-usage contribution/tool-finish goal-progress admission/goal-tool contributor visibility/get_goal/create_goal/update_goal executors and goal-tool dispatch, provider admission, model catalog, turn model/tool planning, model request envelope, model stream route, model stream item reducer/tool input delta/diff consumer/streamed patch parser including sampling error terminal bypass and turn lifecycle, terminal app-server/TUI turn projection, turn replay reconstruction, and pending interactive replay routing, thread snapshot replay dispatch, thread snapshot turn-history replay, thread snapshot collab metadata replay, thread snapshot session refresh, queued rollback overlay sync, rollback response active-queue flush, replayed server-request surface routing, app-server request resolution routing, app-server response dispatch intent, app-server request enqueue routing, app-server queued request delivery, thread buffered request eviction/filtering, thread session-refresh rebase, thread active-turn cache lifecycle, thread side-parent pending status, status-change, side-thread UI sync, side-thread discard cleanup, side-thread fork/start, side-thread startup event routing, side-thread composer handoff, side-thread agent navigation cleanup, async runtime contract pressure slices, and selected fixture-backed adapter pressure slices, but it is not production-ready for broad Codex/Cafex replacement yet.
requiredBeforeBroadReplacement: Broaden haxe.rust parity evidence beyond selected helper/headless/adapter pressure fixtures.
Resolve haxe.rust-362 nullable Array.shift class-return lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-ojj non-copy class field assign-op lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-fzl non-copy local conditional-result reuse lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-3f0g static final class field access path lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-fz20 Reflect.compare lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-3oju optional primitive constructor default lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-akfm generic helper bound propagation or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Resolve haxe.rust-i8li optional anonymous-field read lowering or carry an explicit release-blocking compiler risk.
Complete license/distribution review for haxe.rust GPL-3.0 with Codex/Cafex Apache-2.0 artifacts.
Reduce or explicitly native-own the 4 unsupported Cafex seam rows.
Run replacement rollback drills from the operator runbook.
Add broader live runtime parity evidence for model/provider, TUI, restart, and checkpoint ownership.

scorecard.rationale: The project has kept interop typed and narrow: generated Rust is disposable, haxe.rust stays external, and app/test Haxe source has no raw Rust snippets.
strengths: Raw Rust escape pressure is 0 matches across 760 Haxe source/test files.
haxe.rust fixes are generic and made in ../haxe.rust.
Pin update workflow is scripted and gated.
caveats: Future metal/native boundaries still need real host-effect implementations, including production SQLite/log state.

scorecard.rationale: Process execution, sandbox policy, diagnostics redaction, and mutation controls fail closed in fixture gates, but real platform sandbox enforcement and production operational drills are not complete.
strengths: Process exec wrapper denies by default.
Sandbox gate fails closed for unsupported platforms.
Diagnostics redaction has fixture coverage.
caveats: Real OS sandbox enforcement remains a native boundary.
No production default has changed.
Security/licensing review is still required before distribution.

scorecard.rationale: Generated Rust builds and fixture checks are repeatable, and HXCX-7.5 defines how codexhx pressure should become generic haxe.rust portable/metal convergence benchmarks, but codexhx does not yet have a production runtime performance suite.
strengths: Generated Cargo checks/tests run under locked gates.
Portable-first profile policy avoids premature raw-Rust rewrites.
Generated output is not committed as source.
caveats: No throughput/latency benchmark covers headless turns, tools, or adapters.
Clippy/fmt are advisory rather than required.
Metal-native performance claims need future focused benchmarks.

scorecard.rationale: The pressure-test process has produced upstreamable haxe.rust fixes, checked ledgers, and repeatable pins; broad replacement remains too costly while four generic compiler regressions and Cafex live runtime seams are unsupported.
strengths: 14 pressure gaps resolved upstream rather than carried as private compiler hacks.
Native SQLite pressure uncovered and resolved three generic std metal-clean gaps in haxe.rust.
Native state-adapter pressure uncovered and resolved generic HxRef enum-payload equality in haxe.rust.
The new nullable Array.shift class-return regression is tracked as haxe.rust-362 instead of app-specific compiler code.
The non-copy class field assign-op regression is tracked as haxe.rust-ojj instead of app-specific compiler code.
The non-copy local conditional-result reuse regression is tracked as haxe.rust-fzl instead of app-specific compiler code.
The static final class field access path regression is tracked as haxe.rust-3f0g instead of app-specific compiler code.
The Reflect.compare lowering regression is tracked as haxe.rust-fz20 instead of app-specific compiler code.
Friction comparison supports selected adapter slices and review tooling.
Operator rollback and migration-mode policies are checked.
caveats: Cafex has 4 unsupported seam rows.
haxe.rust/Codex/Cafex drift must continue through pin and fixture gates.

scorecard.rationale: haxe.rust is GPL-3.0 while upstream Codex and Cafex pins are Apache-2.0; the experiment can continue locally, but release/bundling obligations are not reviewed.
strengths: haxe.rust remains an external checkout, not bundled.
Generated Rust is build output and not committed.
Operator runbook blocks production default changes before decision review.
caveats: Binary or bundled distribution needs license review.
Generated artifact obligations are unresolved.
Vendoring haxe.rust is deferred unless a later reproducibility decision and license review accept it.

feedsReplacementReview: Recommend HXCX-6.3 select helper-only, sidecar/headless, or selected adapter-slice mode.
Do not select broad replacement from current evidence.
Keep unsupported surfaces explicit and fail-closed.

Treat haxe.rust-362, haxe.rust-ojj, haxe.rust-fzl, haxe.rust-3f0g, haxe.rust-fz20, haxe.rust-3oju, haxe.rust-akfm, and haxe.rust-i8li as open compiler risks until fixed with product-neutral regressions.
Treat license/distribution as a release blocker, not a local-experiment blocker.
